other_observers: Larry Semo and several others whose names I did not get; Originally found by Joe Mammoser on an earlier date.
description: A small passerine similar in size and shape of Northern Parula. Underparts were mostly lemon yellow from chin to upper belly, blending into white on the belly with white vent region, and with a flame orange chest; upper mandible dark, lower mandible flesh colored; upperparts were basally dark bluish-gray; head also had a solid black eye mask extending from back of eye forward to bill base and down to the yellow throat; the mantle was a solid olive green patch; and the wings had two white wing bars; I did not note any markings or spots on the tail; the eye was dark.Behaviors: Mostly sang from tree tops, occasionally foraging within the tree canopy.Call: very much like Northern Parula, except without the ending sharp notePlumage: adult
